recording_notes: MONITOR RECORDING — phone filming RCA monitor at FLS skills lab. Same setup as V11-V14/V17 (pink/red foam block, perforated white base, Penrose drain). White/light suture — very difficult to track against white background and drain. Monitor glare present at upper edge. Resolution 1136x1078 (non-standard crop, lower than 1920x1080 monitor-recorded V17-V19). NOTABLE: A blue IRWIN-branded instrument appears in the final ~10 seconds of the video — possible external scissors introduced during the cutting phase. This is a significant protocol observation requiring confirmation.

frame_analyses.description: Drain visible on pink foam block with white tape band. Penrose drain centered with yellow/green opening visible. No instruments in field. Two mark dots barely visible on drain — hard to see on monitor recording. Clean starting position.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Setup matches V11-V14/V17 skills lab configuration. No suture debris visible on tape (unlike V19 which had heavy debris from prior attempts). This suggests V21 may be an early attempt in the session.

frame_analyses.description: First instrument entering from upper right. Curved needle with suture visible — dark suture thread extending from instrument tip down toward drain. Second instrument partially visible at upper left. TIMING STARTS approximately 6 seconds.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Instrument entry at typical pace. Suture appears to be pre-loaded on needle — efficient start.

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver approaching drain with loaded needle. Dark suture line visible from tip to drain area. Left instrument entering from upper left. Positioning for needle pass.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle loading phase brief — approximately 8 seconds. Good efficiency.

frame_analyses.description: Active needle pass through drain. Curved needle clearly visible arcing from center through drain tissue. White suture trailing from needle. Left instrument (grasper) stabilizing drain from right side. Both instruments fully engaged.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First needle pass underway. Needle angle appears appropriate. Left instrument providing counter-traction on drain.

frame_analyses.description: Needle driving continues. Both instruments converging on drain. Curved needle visible with white suture extending from both sides of drain — suture passing through first mark. Left instrument holding drain, right instrument driving needle.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Active needle pass. Good bilateral instrument coordination visible.

frame_analyses.description: Needle has passed through drain. Both suture ends now visible extending from drain — one going upper left, one going lower right. Instruments positioned at drain level pulling suture through. Needle visible at instrument tip. Suture placement through both marks nearing completion.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both suture ends visible — needle has been driven through both marks. Pulling suture to equalize lengths.

frame_analyses.description: Suture placement complete. Instruments repositioning above drain. Suture ends visible extending from drain to instruments. Needle visible at upper left held by left instrument. Right instrument holding other suture end. Preparing for first throw wrap.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Suture placement completed at approximately 34-36 seconds (22-24s placement time). Transitioning to first throw. Placement time is EXCELLENT — matches series peak of 22s.

frame_analyses.description: First throw (surgeon's knot) wrapping in progress. Both instruments above drain. Suture being wrapped around one instrument for the double throw. Loop of suture visible between instrument tips. Dark suture line extending from wrap to drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double wrap for surgeon's knot underway. Instruments positioned well for wrapping motion.

frame_analyses.description: First throw tightening. Both instruments at drain level pulling suture tight. Knot beginning to form at drain surface. Both instruments pulling in opposite directions. Suture crossing visible at drain center.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw being cinched down. Good lateral pull technique for knot seating.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw preparation. Both instruments above drain, repositioning for next wrap. Suture loop visible at right side of drain. Dark suture extending upward to instruments. First throw appears complete and seated.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First throw completed at approximately 56-58s (~22s duration). Hand positions appear to have switched — left instrument now leads. Confidence: low.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw wrapping/tightening. Both instruments above drain pulling suture. Multiple suture lines visible. Left instrument grasping suture from left side (consistent with hand switch from first throw). Knot building at drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Second throw in progress. Hand switch appears present. Single wrap visible.

frame_analyses.description: Second throw tightening. Both instruments near drain level. Suture crossed above drain. Left instrument from left, right instrument from right. Knot visible at drain surface.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Second throw completion around 76-78s. Duration: ~20 seconds. Efficient.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw in progress. Left instrument from upper left. Right instrument from lower right. Suture wrapping visible with loop at right side. Knot mass building up at drain center.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw wrapping. Hand switch from second throw appears present.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw continuing. Both instruments near knot. Large suture loops visible on and around drain surface. Multiple wraps evident. Instruments pulling and tightening.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw extended — still working at 92s. Large suture loops suggest possible difficulty or additional wrapping.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw tightening. Both instruments at drain pulling. Knot visible at drain center. Drain slightly pinched/compressed by knot tension. Suture ends visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good knot tension evident from drain compression. 22+ seconds into third throw — extended.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw completing. Both instruments at drain. Knot seated. Suture tails extending. Green highlight visible at knot area on monitor.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw completing at ~108-110s. Duration: ~32 seconds — longest single throw. Total throw time: 74 seconds.

frame_analyses.description: Scissors clearly visible — serrated edge of laparoscopic scissors from upper left. Text/markings on instrument shaft. Right instrument at drain. Positioned near first suture tail.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Cutting phase underway. Laparoscopic scissors identified. First cut positioning at ~120s. Cutting started approximately 110-115s.

frame_analyses.description: Active cutting. Scissors at lower left of drain. Knot visible at center with suture wraps. One tail being cut. Second instrument providing counter-traction.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: First cut in progress. 18+ seconds into cutting phase.

frame_analyses.description: Both instruments widely separated. Knot visible at drain center. Suture tails splayed outward, still relatively long. Drain slit appears mostly CLOSED around knot — good approximation visible.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: 40+ seconds into cutting. Tails still long. Drain closure confirmed at this point — good quality.

frame_analyses.description: Single instrument visible from upper left. Knot clearly visible with crossing pattern — compact. Tails present but shorter than at 150s. Progress being made on cuts.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: 54+ seconds of cutting. Some progress on tail shortening.

frame_analyses.description: NOTABLE: Blue IRWIN-branded instrument now visible from lower right alongside gray laparoscopic instrument. Both at drain. Knot visible. White suture tails still present.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: PROTOCOL FLAG: Non-laparoscopic IRWIN tool appears. Cannot confirm if used for cutting from monitor view.

frame_analyses.description: Final frame. Blue IRWIN and gray instruments at drain. Knot compact with short stubs. Drain slit mostly closed. Drain not avulsed, centered on block. Task completing as video ends at 177.4s.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Video ends with instruments present. Completion estimated at 170-175s. Knot intact, drain closure adequate.

knot_assessments.notes: First throw 36-58s (22s). Right instrument leads wrap. Double wrap visible at 44s consistent with surgeon's knot. Tightened by 52-56s. Confidence: low — monitor recording, white suture.
Second throw 58-78s (20s). Left instrument leads (hand switch confirmed). Single wrap. Efficient throw. Confidence: low.

Third throw 78-110s (32s) — LONGEST single throw. Right instrument leads (hand switch confirmed). Extended duration with large loops at 92s. May indicate difficulty grasping or wrapping. Confidence: low.

suture_placement.placement_notes: Marks barely visible on monitor. Suture appears to pass through correct area at 28s. Both ends exit drain at appropriate positions. 2mm per mark is a conservative estimate. Direct camera would be needed for accurate measurement.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: At 150s, drain slit appears mostly closed around knot — good approximation. Knot compact at 164-175s. No visible gap from monitor view. Confidence LOW. Suture tails moderate length — not as long as V20's catastrophic tails. Drain centered on block, not avulsed.

protocol_observations.description: Blue IRWIN-branded instrument appears in camera field during final ~10s (165-177s). Appears to be external tool — possibly scissors or pliers — rather than standard laparoscopic instrument.
protocol_observations.impact_on_scoring: Cannot confirm from monitor whether tool was used for cutting or was incidental. No penalty applied but flagged. If external scissors were used, the effective laparoscopic cutting time was ~55s with the final cuts done externally.
protocol_observations.recommendation: Confirm with trainee. If external tools were used, correct for future attempts — FLS exam requires all cutting inside trainer with laparoscopic instruments.

technique_summary: V21 at an estimated 170 seconds is a moderate recovery from the V18-V20 fatigue crash sequence. This is a MONITOR RECORDING at the skills lab. Suture placement at 22 seconds is EXCELLENT — matching the V14 series peak and dramatically better than V19 (43s) and V20 (40s), confirming placement regression was fatigue-related not skill loss. Throws at 74 seconds total are mid-range (peak: 48s), with the third throw at 32 seconds being the longest phase. The CUTTING PHASE at 65 seconds (37% of total time) matches V19's worst-ever and continues the series-wide structural bottleneck. A blue IRWIN-branded external instrument appears in the final frames — possible protocol deviation. The absence of suture debris on the tape suggests this is an early session attempt, not a late-session fatigue crash. Overall, V21 demonstrates solid fundamental technique retention with a persistent cutting bottleneck that requires dedicated isolated practice.
improvement_suggestions: CUTTING IS THE #1 STRUCTURAL BOTTLENECK: 65s (37% of time). This matches V19's worst and has not improved in 7 videos. It is NOT just fatigue — it persists even in fresh attempts. Dedicated cutting drills are CRITICAL.
PROTOCOL: Clarify the blue IRWIN instrument — if external scissors were used, correct this for FLS exam preparation. All cutting must be done laparoscopically inside the trainer.
DEDICATED CUTTING DRILL: Set up pre-tied knots with tails and practice ONLY the cutting phase. Target: 20+ cuts per session, aiming for sub-30s per cut.
Suture placement at 22s is MASTERED at current level — no further drill needed for this phase.
Third throw at 32s was the longest single throw — practice maintaining consistent throw speed, targeting 15-18s per throw.
Return to DIRECT CAMERA recording — monitor recording confidence (0.38) is too low for reliable scoring and progression tracking.
Session discipline appears good if this was attempt #1 — maintain 2-attempt cap per session.
strengths: BEST suture placement in 6 videos: 22 seconds — matches V14 series peak, dramatically better than V19 (43s) and V20 (40s)
All three throws completed in correct sequence (surgeon's knot → single → single)
Hand switches appear present between all throws
Drain not avulsed — safe tissue handling maintained across 21 videos
Drain closure appears adequate — no visible gap
Clean tape (no debris) suggests early-session freshness, good session discipline
14% overall improvement from pre-training baseline maintained (198s → 170s)

phase_comparison.placement_22s: BEST in 6 videos — matches V14 peak. V19: 43s, V20: 40s. Confirms regression was fatigue.
phase_comparison.throws_74s: Mid-range. V14 peak: 48s. V20: 80s. Third throw at 32s is the drag.
phase_comparison.cutting_65s: STRUCTURAL BOTTLENECK. V14 best: 29s. V19: 65s. V20: 83s. No improvement trend in 7 videos.

key_observations: 170s / 424 FLS — mid-range recovery, 48s faster than V20 (218s), 63s slower than V14 peak (107s)
RETURN TO MONITOR RECORDING — confidence 0.38 (from V20's 0.60 direct camera)
Suture placement RESTORED to peak: 22s — confirms V19/V20 regression was fatigue, not skill loss
Cutting 65s (37% of time) — matches V19's worst. STRUCTURAL bottleneck confirmed — not just fatigue
Throws 74s total — mid-range, improved from V20 (80s), far from peak (48s)
ANOMALY: Blue IRWIN instrument in final frames — possible external scissors. Protocol deviation flagged.
Clean tape suggests early session attempt — no fatigue. 170s may be true rested baseline at this training stage.
21st video in series — patterns well established. Cutting is THE priority for further improvement.
Fresh-session baseline trajectory: V7 142s → V11 135s → V15 142s → V17 158s → V21 170s — slight upward drift suggests muscle memory is not consolidating between sessions for cutting
